description: Safety rules for genetic medicines - off-target prediction, genotoxicity, and toxicology markers.

facts.context: CRISPR off-target editing is predicted by:
facts.truth: guide RNA seed region similarity in PAM-proximal 10-12nt
distractors: stochastic chromatin accessibility at random genomic loci
exact full-length complementarity across all 20 nucleotides
transgene expression level in the target cell population

facts.context: Unbiased off-target detection methods like GUIDE-seq work by:
facts.truth: capturing genome-wide DSBs via tagged oligo integration
distractors: amplifying only computationally predicted candidate sites
measuring total cellular guide RNA abundance by qPCR
quantifying downstream protein expression fold changes

facts.context: High-fidelity Cas9 variants (eSpCas9, HiFi) reduce off-targets by:
facts.truth: weakening non-specific DNA contacts for stricter complementarity
distractors: accelerating cleavage kinetics uniformly at every target site
extending PAM recognition from 3bp to 6bp specificity window
increasing nuclear localization signal copy number per enzyme

facts.context: Insertional mutagenesis from lentiviral vectors risks:
facts.truth: oncogene activation or tumor suppressor disruption at insertion
distractors: transient episomal expression without genomic alteration events
selective toxicity restricted to post-mitotic non-dividing cells
uniform transgene silencing across all chromosomal integration loci

facts.context: AAV vectors are non-integrating, but residual integration:
facts.truth: occurs rarely, enriched at double-strand break sites
distractors: reaches high frequency comparable to lentiviral vector rates
happens exclusively in prokaryotic bacterial host genomes
distributes uniformly across all chromosomal regions equally

facts.context: CRISPR double-strand breaks activate p53, causing:
facts.truth: cell cycle arrest and selection for p53-mutant cells
distractors: accelerated proliferation of edited cell populations overall
enhanced homology-directed repair efficiency at cut sites
immediate terminal differentiation of all transfected cells

facts.context: Cytokine release syndrome from gene therapy features:
facts.truth: elevated IL-6 and TNF-alpha with systemic organ dysfunction
distractors: decreased inflammatory markers with localized injection reaction
selective IFN-beta elevation without hemodynamic instability noted
transient complement activation resolving before clinical symptoms

facts.context: High-dose AAV hepatotoxicity is monitored by:
facts.truth: elevated ALT/AST transaminases requiring corticosteroid management
distractors: serum creatinine and blood urea nitrogen renal function panels
peripheral blood smear morphology for hemolytic anemia markers
cerebrospinal fluid protein levels for neuroinflammation screening

facts.context: AAV gene therapy thrombocytopenia is driven by:
facts.truth: complement activation and thrombotic microangiopathy at high doses
distractors: direct megakaryocyte suppression in bone marrow progenitor niches
autoimmune platelet destruction via anti-GPIIb/IIIa antibody binding
splenic sequestration from capsid-induced splenomegaly at all doses

facts.context: AAV dorsal root ganglion toxicity occurs in:
facts.truth: high-dose systemic or intrathecal delivery causing neuron loss
distractors: low-dose local injection restricted to specific transgene cassettes
oral administration routes affecting peripheral motor neuron axons
all delivery methods equally regardless of dose or administration
